item.title: U.S. CLARITY Act Senate vote depends on seven Democrats; House Financial Services schedules July 17 digital asset market-structure hearing.
EU MiCA transition deadline expires July 1; 53 firms licensed with Tether and Binance excluded from European crypto markets.
SEC plans to propose rule exempting crypto companies from securities registration requirements as soon as July 2026.
SEC moves to eliminate 20-year-old rule restricting Wall Street firms from using blockchain-based trading and settlement infrastructure.
UK FCA publishes final crypto regulatory framework promising cross-border trading access; industry warns of substantial compliance barriers.
JPMorgan studies Bitcoin-backed collateral program that analysts estimate could unlock $20 billion in institutional liquidity.
JPMorgan plans to offer direct crypto trading to clients, extending blockchain-native services to institutional customer base.
BNY Mellon enables institutions to mint and redeem USDC directly, deepening stablecoin infrastructure partnership with Circle.
Summer.fi halts all vaults after flash loan attack drains $6 million from USDC yield pools on July 6.
Ethical hackers disclose Aptos blockchain flaw that could have exposed $70 billion in assets; patch deployed with no funds lost.
Vanguard posts digital assets leadership role covering tokenization, stablecoins, and blockchain strategy for its 50 million investors.
Dinari and tZERO launch turnkey tokenized U.S. equities platform targeting brokerages and financial institutions.
ESMA's list of licensed EU crypto service providers grows to 280 as MiCA full enforcement begins.
Circle becomes first stablecoin issuer to receive full MiCA regulatory authorization in the European Union.
Ripple secures full MiCA authorization, adding to its portfolio of more than 75 regulatory approvals across global markets.
